explicit_text: The Governor's Council on Human Relations in Georgia was established by Governor Jimmy Carter in October 1971. I served as the coordinator of this council, which had equal black and white participation and aimed to foster communication and mediate racial issues across the state. Although it lacked enforcement powers and a separate budget, it played a vital role in opening dialogue and supporting biracial councils in Georgia's counties.
